beak.experimental.preparation module

beak.experimental.preparation._get_unique_values_list(array: ndarray, include_nan: bool = False, include_inf: bool = False, verbose=0) → Tuple[Sequence[Number], Sequence[Number]][source]

Get a list of unique values and their counts from a numpy array.

Parameters:

  • array (np.ndarray) – The input array from which to extract unique values.

  • include_nan (bool, optional) – Whether to include NaN values in the unique values list. Defaults to False.

  • verbose (int, optional) – Whether to print the unique values and their counts. Defaults to 0 (no output).

Returns:

A tuple containing two numpy arrays:

  • classes: The unique values in the array.

  • counts: The counts of each unique value in the array.

Return type:

np.ndarray

beak.experimental.preparation._sampling_by_selection(file: Path | str, include: Sequence[int] | None = None, exclude: Sequence[int] | None = None)[source]

Select pixels from a raster file based on inclusion and exclusion criteria.

Parameters:

  • file (Union[Path, str]) – The path to the raster file.

  • include (Sequence[int], optional) – A sequence of values to include in the selection. Defaults to None.

  • exclude (Optional[Sequence[int]], optional) – A sequence of values to exclude from the selection. Defaults to None.

Returns:

  • The selected pixels as a numpy array.

  • A dictionary containing the settings used for selection and final results.

Return type:

Tuple[np.ndarray, Dict[str, Union[str, Number, Sequence[int]]]]

Raises:

ValueError – If the include and exclude parameters are identical.

beak.experimental.preparation._sampling_by_threshold(file: Path | str, type: Literal['positives', 'negatives'], threshold: Number, exclude: Sequence[int] | None = None)[source]

Select pixels from a raster based on a given threshold and type.

Parameters:

  • file (Union[Path, str]) – The path to the raster file.

  • type (Literal["positives", "negatives"]) – The type of pixels to select. “positives” selects pixels greater than or equal to the threshold. “negatives” selects pixels less than the threshold.

  • threshold (Number) – The threshold value for selecting pixels.

  • exclude (Optional[Sequence[int]]) – A sequence of values to exclude from selection. Defaults to None.

Returns:

  • The selected pixels as a numpy array.

  • A dictionary containing the settings used for selection and final results.

Return type:

Tuple[np.ndarray, Dict[str, Union[str, Number, Sequence[int]]]]

Raises:

ValueError – If the threshold is higher than the maximum value in the raster, or if the threshold is lower than the minimum value in the raster, or if the threshold is lower than 0.

beak.experimental.preparation._sampling_select_classes(array: ndarray, **kwargs)[source]
beak.experimental.preparation._sampling_select_random_points(array: ndarray, selection: Sequence[int], strategy: Sequence[Number] | Number, target_value: int | None = None, merge_classes: bool = False, min_px: int = 1, seed: int | None = None) → Tuple[ndarray, dict][source]

Select random points from a given array based on the specified strategy and method.

Parameters:

  • array (np.ndarray) – The input array from which to select points.

  • selection (Sequence[int]) – The sequence of values to include in the class selection.

  • strategy (Union[Sequence[Number], Number]) – The sampling strategy, either a single value or a sequence of values. Values must be between 0 and 1, representing the fraction of pixels to select. If not a single value, values must be provided for each class in the same order as the classes.

  • target_value (int) – The value to assign to the selected points.

  • merge_classes (bool) – The method to use for sampling. Defaults to False. “True” will merge all pixels of the selected classes to one value before sampling. “False” will sample the pixels in each cluster separately.

  • min_px (int, optional) – The minimum number of pixels to select for each class. Defaults to 1.

  • seed (Optional[int], optional) – The seed for the random number generator. Defaults to None.

Returns:

  • A numpy array with the selected points.

  • A dictionary with most important information about the sampling process and results.

Return type:

Tuple[np.ndarray, dict]

Raises:

  • ValueError – If the strategy values are not between 0 and 1.

  • ValueError – If the length of the strategy values does not match the number of classes.

  • ValueError – If the provided array does not contain any valid values.

  • ValueError – If the provided selection and the array classes do not match.

beak.experimental.preparation.create_encodings_from_dataframe(value_columns: List[str], data: DataFrame, export_absent: bool) → Tuple[DataFrame, List[str]][source]

Create encodings for categorical data.

Parameters:

  • value_columns (List[str]) – List of column names containing categorical values.

  • data (pd.DataFrame) – Input data frame.

  • export_absent (bool) – Flag indicating whether to export “Absent” columns.

Returns:

Encoded data frame. List[str]: List of new column names.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

beak.experimental.preparation.create_hard_buffer_around_labels(array: ndarray, radius: int = 1, shape: Literal['square', 'circle'] = 'circle', target_value: int = 1, buffer_value: Number | None = None, overwrite_nodata: bool = False) → ndarray[source]

Extends the positive values in the given array.

A: If the buffer value is not specified, the buffer value will be the same as the positive labels value. B: If the buffer value is specified, elements around the positive labels will be replaced with this value.

For extending positive labels, use option A. For excluding negative labels, use option B with values such as np.nan oder -1.

Parameters:

  • array (np.ndarray) – The array to be extended.

  • radius (int) – The radius of the buffer (1 for a 3x3 window with 9 pixels, size is n*2 + 1). Defaults to 1.

  • shape (Literal["square", "circle"]) – The shape of the buffer. Defaults to “square”.

  • target_value (int) – The value to be extended. Defaults to 1.

  • buffer_value (Optional[Number]) – The value to be used for the buffer. Defaults to None.

  • overwrite_nodata (bool) – Whether to extend the buffer into nodata cells.

Returns:

The extended label’s array.

Return type:

np.ndarray

beak.experimental.preparation.delete_nan_elements(array: ndarray, transpose: bool = True) → ndarray[source]

Deletes NaN elements from the given array.

Parameters:

  • array (np.ndarray) – The array to be processed.

  • transpose (bool, optional) – Flag indicating whether to transpose the array before processing. Defaults to True.

Returns:

The processed array with NaN elements removed.

Return type:

np.ndarray

beak.experimental.preparation.impute_data(data: ndarray | DataFrame | GeoDataFrame, columns: List[str] | None = None, strategy: Literal['mean', 'median', 'most_frequent', 'constant'] = 'mean', fill_value: Number | str | None = None, missing_values: Number | None = nan) → ndarray | DataFrame | GeoDataFrame[source]

Imputes missing values in the given data using the specified strategy.

For arrays, the data needs to be in the shape (rows, columns), e.g. 3 layers with 5 entries each: (5, 3).

Parameters:

  • data (Union[np.ndarray, pd.DataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame]) – The data to be imputed. NoData values must already by set to NaN.

  • columns (List[str]) – The columns to be imputed.

  • strategy (str, optional) – The imputation strategy. Defaults to “mean”. Choose “constant” and provide a fill value if targeted for imputing with min or max values.

  • fill_value (Union[Number, str], optional) – The value to fill missing values with. Defaults to None.

  • missing_values (Optional[Number], optional) – The value to be treated as missing. Defaults to np.nan.

Returns:

The imputed data.

Return type:

Union[np.ndarray, pd.DataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame]
